AdaptationContribution.description:
Per-session adaptation signal by capacity axis.

Computed by the dose engine from aggregated phi_adapt vectors and session
load. Consumed by state_update to drive explicit capacity gains.
AdaptationContribution.title: AdaptationContribution

AdherenceMetrics.description: Recent plan adherence and the current training streak.

pct.description: completed / scheduled over the window, 0-100; None if nothing scheduled
pct.title: Pct

streak_days.description: consecutive days with a completed session / logged workout
streak_days.title: Streak Days

window_days.description: length of the adherence window in days
window_days.title: Window Days

AdherenceMetrics.title: AdherenceMetrics

AxisProjection.description: Projected trajectory for a single capacity axis under plan + baseline.

baseline.description: Value at week N under the maintain plan
baseline.title: Baseline

projected.description: Value at week N under this plan
projected.title: Projected

AxisProjection.title: AxisProjection

CapacityConfidence.description:
Per-axis model uncertainty about ``CapacityState``, as a variance proxy.

Higher variance ⇒ the model is less sure ⇒ a benchmark corrects that axis more
(scalar Kalman gain). The seed prior is high-variance — a weak prior that yields
to data — benchmarks shrink it and time grows it. Tracked for capacity axes only
(fatigue/tissue are transient, re-driven each session). See ADR-0036; the scalar
here generalizes to the EKF's covariance later (ADR-0015).
CapacityConfidence.title: CapacityConfidence

CapacityState.description: X_t — capacity / adaptation ceiling components (higher = better).
CapacityState.title: CapacityState

ConnectionStatus.description: Returned by GET /connection whether or not a connection exists.
ConnectionStatus.title: ConnectionStatus

DashboardBundleOut.description: Latest primary-anchor observations plus derived KPI snapshots.
DashboardBundleOut.title: DashboardBundleOut

ExerciseCatalogOut.description: The fields the log UI needs to render a catalog-bound set entry.
ExerciseCatalogOut.title: ExerciseCatalogOut

ExerciseEntry.description:
A single exercise within a session log.

`phi_*` and `energy_mix` are resolved from the Exercise DB row by the
service layer before the dose engine runs. They are left blank in
client-submitted logs; the engine falls back to modality defaults when absent.
ExerciseEntry.title: ExerciseEntry

ExercisePrescription.description: A single prescribed exercise within a session.

e1rm_basis_kg.description: The current e1RM the suggestion was resolved against.
e1rm_basis_kg.title: E1Rm Basis Kg

percent_e1rm.description: Fraction of estimated 1RM the suggested load targets (0–1).
percent_e1rm.title: Percent E1Rm

prescribed_load_kg.description: Suggested working load in kg (pre-fills the log).
prescribed_load_kg.title: Prescribed Load Kg

rpe_cap.description: Upper RPE bound for the working sets (ADR-0029 envelope).
rpe_cap.title: Rpe Cap

ExercisePrescription.title: ExercisePrescription

ExternalIntensity.description:
The session-scalar external intensity ``I`` that entered the dose base (ADR-0039).

Model A: a weighted session-level intensity replaces the old hardcoded ``1.0``.
``value`` is what the dose law raised to ``dose_alpha``; ``fallback_path`` names
the rung that dominated; ``known_limitation`` records the ADR-0054 routing caveat
(a session scalar shapes every exercise via the aggregate-φ path, so a hard
accessory partially inherits the session's intensity).
ExternalIntensity.title: ExternalIntensity

FatigueState.description: F_t — multi-component fatigue (0–100). Higher = more fatigued.
FatigueState.title: FatigueState

IntensityContribution.description:
One exercise's contribution to the session external intensity (ADR-0039).

Carries the intensity value **and its provenance** so the dose is auditable: the
e1RM denominator that produced a ``relative_load`` reading (value + semantics +
the observation it came from), which ladder rung was taken, and the aggregation
weight (``w = reps · load``).

source.description: Ladder rung: relative_load | rpe_rir_chart | epley_failure | neutral_missing
source.title: Source

IntensityContribution.title: IntensityContribution

next_step.default: Call GET /v1/next-session?goal=Strength to get first prescription
next_step.title: Next Step

OverviewMetrics.description: Real dashboard tiles: training load / ACWR and adherence / streak.
OverviewMetrics.title: OverviewMetrics

PrescriptionExplanation.description: Why this session — state drivers, constraints, sources.

prescription_branch.description: Internal prescriber branch id (safety, readiness, goal path)
prescription_branch.title: Prescription Branch

score.description: Template-aligned fit score vs twin state (0–1)
score.title: Score

structured_template_name.description: Display name for structured coaching template (v2)
structured_template_name.title: Structured Template Name

PrescriptionExplanation.title: PrescriptionExplanation

ProfileUpdate.description:
Partial update — only fields present in the request body are written.

Nullable fields (lifts, biometrics) accept an explicit ``null`` to clear a
previously stored value; omitting a field leaves it untouched.
ProfileUpdate.title: ProfileUpdate

ProgressBlock.description:
Direction-aware progress toward a benchmark-linked objective's target.

``current``/``pct``/``direction`` are all null for a free-text objective
(no linked benchmark) — countdown-only via ``days_to_go`` on the parent.
ProgressBlock.title: ProgressBlock

ProjectionRequest.description: Parameters for a forward projection under a hypothetical plan.

weekly_volume.description: Relative weekly volume index (30..90, matches the web slider)
weekly_volume.title: Weekly Volume

ProjectionRequest.title: ProjectionRequest

ProjectionResponse.description: Full projection: 8 axes (canonical order) + readiness + peak fatigue.

readiness_series.description: Length weeks+1, overall readiness 0..100 under this plan
readiness_series.title: Readiness Series

ProjectionResponse.title: ProjectionResponse

ReadinessComponent.description: How a single acute-wellness signal nudged readiness.

baseline.description: Personal rolling baseline, or the default anchor
baseline.title: Baseline

contribution.description: Direction-signed, clamped deviation in [-1, 1] (1 = a full unit better than baseline)
contribution.title: Contribution

ReadinessComponent.title: ReadinessComponent

ReadinessConfidence.description:
How well-supported today's readiness estimate is (ADR-0052).

``confidence`` answers "how much evidence supports the score", NOT "is readiness high".
Evidence-coverage over load / wellness-signal coverage / freshness / baseline maturity.
ReadinessConfidence.title: ReadinessConfidence

kpi_flags.description: Soft signals from KPIs (e.g. elevated run fatigue factor)
kpi_flags.title: Kpi Flags

ReadinessScore.description:
The one backend-owned readiness number (PDR-0005) + its breakdown.

``score`` is ``None`` only when there is no modeled ``AthleteState`` to anchor against —
wellness modulates the model, it is not a standalone score. ``confidence`` reports how
well-supported that score is (ADR-0052) and is report-only in P8.

as_of.description: Timestamp of the modeled state used
as_of.title: As Of

wellness_delta.description: Signed acute-wellness adjustment applied, in 0–100 points (0 if no sample)
wellness_delta.title: Wellness Delta

ReadinessScore.title: ReadinessScore

RecommendationGate.description:
Advisory recommendation authority derived from confidence (ADR-0052).

**Report-only in P8:** ``enforced`` is always ``False`` and the prescriber does not
consume this — confidence cannot gate the plan yet (that is P13). It is displayed and
logged so P13 inherits shadow history. Distinct from the readiness *score*, which may
transparently nudge the plan.

enforced.description: Always False in P8 — see ADR-0052
enforced.title: Enforced

RecommendationGate.title: RecommendationGate

SessionFeedbackIn.description:
Athlete-reported outcome for one planned session.

``planned_session_id`` must belong to the caller; ``completed_workout_log_id``
(when given) must too — both are validated server-side to prevent IDOR.
SessionFeedbackIn.title: SessionFeedbackIn

SignalSummary.description:
Honesty-ladder buckets over the athlete's logical wellness signals (ADR-0053).

``provided`` measured today; ``unknown_today`` tracked but absent today (a gap,
penalizes confidence, never imputed); ``untracked`` not expected (hidden, no penalty);
``stale`` last sample is not fresh; ``estimated`` reserved for a future carry-forward
promotion (always empty in P8 — ADR-0049 ships clean gaps, not imputation).
SignalSummary.title: SignalSummary

StressDose.description:
Stress dose: six-dimensional engine vector plus legacy scalars for clients.

`adaptation_contribution` is the session's positive adaptation signal per
capacity axis. Used by state_update for explicit capacity gains.
StressDose.title: StressDose

StressDoseSix.description: D_t — session stress dose in six dimensions (non-negative).
StressDoseSix.title: StressDoseSix

TrainingLoadMetrics.description:
Acute:chronic workload ratio vs the 0.8-1.3 sweet spot.

``acute`` is the 7-day summed load; ``chronic`` is the average *weekly*
load over 28 days (28-day sum / 4). ``acwr`` = acute / chronic. All three
are ``None`` when there is insufficient history to compute a meaningful
baseline (``status == "insufficient"``).

acwr.description: acute(7d) / chronic(28d avg weekly) load ratio
acwr.title: Acwr

TrainingLoadMetrics.title: TrainingLoadMetrics

UnifiedStateVector.description:
Digital twin snapshot S(t): legacy scalars + decomposed engine vectors.

Legacy fields remain for backward-compatible API clients; they are derived
from (capacity_x, fatigue_f, tissue_t) on each update.
UnifiedStateVector.title: UnifiedStateVector

WearableConnectionOut.description: Public view of a wearable connection — no tokens, ever.
WearableConnectionOut.title: WearableConnectionOut

WeekProgress.description:
Cross-block schedule position, derived from the macrocycle's
``start_date`` and the anchor Objective's ``target_date`` — never stored.

- ``current_week`` is 1-indexed and always present (open-ended programs
  still have a "week N"). It is capped at ``total_weeks`` when the horizon
  is known, so it never reads "week 9 of 8".
- ``total_weeks``/``pct``/``weeks_to_go`` are null when the anchor has no
  ``target_date`` (or a target on/before the start) — an open horizon.
WeekProgress.title: WeekProgress

WellnessSampleIn.description: One acute daily-wellness reading to ingest (manual entry or provider pull).
WellnessSampleIn.title: WellnessSampleIn

benchmark_results.description: Optional benchmark key/value payload for benchmark sessions.
benchmark_results.title: Benchmark Results

dominant_movement_pattern.description: e.g. squat | hinge | run — defaults inferred from modality
dominant_movement_pattern.title: Dominant Movement Pattern

estimated_sets.description: If set, refines volume term in dose law
estimated_sets.title: Estimated Sets

exercises.description: Per-exercise breakdown. When present, dose reflects actual exercise phi vectors.
exercises.title: Exercises

life_stress_inverse.description: 1 = Very high life stress, 10 = No life stress
life_stress_inverse.title: Life Stress Inverse

novelty.description: >1 = novel / high coordination demand for this athlete
novelty.title: Novelty

planned_session_id.description: If provided, marks this log as fulfillment of the planned session.
planned_session_id.title: Planned Session Id

sets.description: Per-set log rows. When present, the session is a heterogeneous bag of sets and modality is derived (uniform → that modality, else Mixed).
sets.title: Sets

WorkoutLogSummary.description:
A logged workout, trimmed to what history views render (recent sessions,
weekly training load).
WorkoutLogSummary.title: WorkoutLogSummary

WorkoutPrescription.description: Next-session recommendation. Legacy fields required; `why` optional for old clients.
WorkoutPrescription.title: WorkoutPrescription

WorkoutSetEntry.description:
A single logged set — the atomic unit of a workout (ADR-0045).

Binds to a catalog ``Exercise`` (by ``exercise_id`` or ``exercise_name``); the
exercise's ``load_type`` types which fields are meaningful. Movements not yet in
the catalog log via ``free_text_name`` (no benchmark linkage). ``sets`` is a
quick-entry multiplier: ``3×5 @ 100kg @ RPE8`` is one entry with ``sets=3`` that
the service materializes into three editable ``workout_set_logs`` rows, so per-set
RPE and the top set survive instead of being averaged away.

free_text_name.description: Fallback name for a movement not in the catalog (no benchmark linkage).
free_text_name.title: Free Text Name

is_top_set.description: Force this as the exercise's top set; else the service infers it (heaviest set) to drive e1RM extraction.
is_top_set.title: Is Top Set

load_type.description: Overrides the catalog load_type snapshot; usually left to the service.
load_type.title: Load Type

sets.description: Quick-entry multiplier: materialize this many identical set rows.
sets.title: Sets

WorkoutSetEntry.title: WorkoutSetEntry

schema.title: Response Get Run Program Program Run Get
Response Get Strength Program Program Strength Get

get.description:
The one domain-filtered assessment surface (ADR-0047), with a measurement-debt
ranking of which benchmarks to assess next. ``mode`` is product framing only —
onboarding (``onramp``) vs ongoing (``retest``); the data path is identical.

schema.title: Response Get Benchmark Definitions V1 Benchmarks Definitions Get
Response Get Benchmark Observations V1 Benchmarks Observations Get

get.description:
Real Overview tiles: training load / ACWR and adherence / streak.

Returns null/insufficient figures rather than erroring for users with
little or no history.
List the movement catalog, optionally filtered — powers the log's exercise picker.

schema.title: Response List Exercises V1 Exercises Get

get.description: Return the Oura OAuth authorize URL for the web app to open.

post.description: Connect using an Oura Personal Access Token (single-user fast path).
Pull the caller's Oura data now (on-demand; the cron does this nightly).

schema.title: Response List Macrocycles V1 Macrocycles Get

get.description: DEV-friendly version that auto-initializes baseline state.

parameters.description: Training goal to prescribe for; defaults to the athlete's stored primary goal, then Strength.
parameters.name: goal

schema.description: Training goal to prescribe for; defaults to the athlete's stored primary goal, then Strength.
schema.title: Goal
Response List Objectives V1 Objectives Get
Response List Blocks V1 Planning Blocks Get
Response List Sessions V1 Planning Sessions Get
Response Get Shadow Summary V1 Shadow Summary Get

post.description:
Project the athlete's capacity axes forward under a hypothetical plan.

Loads (or seeds) the caller's current state, then runs the pure projection
engine. No state is written — this is a compute-and-return preview.

get.description: The athlete's recent state vectors, oldest→newest (chart order).

schema.title: Response State History V1 State History Get

get.description:
Return all weak-point rows for the current user.

By default only active (unresolved) rows are returned.
Pass active_only=false to include resolved rows.

schema.title: Response List Weak Points V1 Weak Points  Get

delete.description:
Hard-delete a weak-point row owned by the current user.

Returns 204 No Content on success, 404 if not found or wrong user.

patch.description:
Update confidence, note, and/or resolved_at on a weak-point row.

Only fields explicitly present in the request body are applied.
Sending resolved_at=null re-opens a resolved weak point.

schema.title: Response List Wellness V1 Wellness Get

get.description: The athlete's logged workouts, most recent first.

schema.title: Response List Workouts V1 Workouts Get
